title: Frontend TypeScript compilation errors preventing Docker build
description: The frontend Docker build failed with multiple TypeScript errors that prevented the Vue 3 app from compiling.

errors.error: TS2322: Type 'Promise<AxiosResponse<T>>' is not assignable to type 'Promise<T>'
errors.cause: The axios response interceptor in client.ts unwraps response.data, but the TypeScript generic on apiClient.post<T>() still returns AxiosResponse<T>. The return type didn't match the declared Promise<LoginResponse | TokenPair | AuthUser>.
errors.fix: Cast return values using 'as unknown as Promise<T>' to match the runtime behavior where the interceptor already unwraps response.data.

errors.error: TS6192: All imports in import declaration are unused
errors.cause: Imported 'Classroom' and 'Anomaly' types from '../api/classroom' but never used them — event payload types were defined locally in the same file.

errors.error: TS6133: 'computed' is declared but its value is never read
errors.cause: Imported 'computed' from 'vue' but never used it in the component's <script setup>.

errors: TS6133: 'reactive' is declared but its value is never read
TS6133: 'lotId' is declared but its value is never read
TS6133: 'res' is declared but its value is never read
errors.cause: Three unused declarations: (1) 'reactive' imported from Vue but unused, (2) 'lotId' parameter in showAlertsFor() not referenced, (3) 'res' variable from parkingAlertApi.close() assigned but never read.
errors.fix: Removed 'reactive' from import, prefixed unused param as '_lotId', and removed the unused 'res' variable assignment.

rootCause: Strict TypeScript compilation (vue-tsc) caught type mismatches and unused declarations that were not flagged during development but blocked the production Docker build.
impact: Frontend Docker image could not be built, blocking the entire docker compose up workflow.
